Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Matters Admitted
Off Calendar Mar-03-2025 Advanced From Mar-24-25 To Mar-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2025 Continued To Nov-10-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Matters Admitted
Off Calendar Mar-13-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS GREYSTAR CALIFORNIA, INC., GDCV MARKET STREET 87, LLC's Motion To Deem Matters Admitted.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Aimee Silver failed to serve timely responses to defendant Greystar California Inc.'s requests for admission. In addition her responses were unverified. (Recendez Dec. para. 7.) After Greystar filed its moving papers seeking an order establishing admissions and sanctions, Silver served verified responses. (Silver Dec. para. 3 & Exh. A.)

Silver waived objections by failing to serve timely responses. CCP 2023.280(a). However, Greystar's motion for deemed admissions is denied because Silver has served substantially compliant, verified responses in advance of the hearing. CCP 2033.280(c). Although she recites objections, she provides substantive responses to each RFA.

Greystar does not seek sanctions in its motion, but sanctions in this circumstance are mandatory, even where a party has substantially complied by the hearing date. (CCP 2033.280(c) ["It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion."].) The court awards sanctions in the amount of $100 payable by Silver to Greystar within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
